column3: Recognize sight words and identify CVCC pattern words.
Read CCVC words and recognize diphthong sounds.
Divide multi-syllable words using VCV and VCCV patterns.

column2: Q2 Sight Words in Community Sentences
column3: Recognize and use sight words in community-themed sentences.
Apply the VCV rule to divide words and identify long vowels.
Apply the VCCV rule to divide words and identify short vowels.

column2: Narrative Sight Words: Action and Sequence

Use descriptive and quantity sight words correctly.
Use comparison sight words in sentences.

column3: Use quantity and comparison words logically.

column2: Abstract Sight Words and Spatial Directions
column3: Use directional and abstract sight words.

column3: Demonstrate mastery of all phonics concepts.
Identify and read high-frequency words accurately.
Use vocabulary words related to regional and national themes.
Use content-specific vocabulary for Math and Science.

column3: Identify and classify common and proper nouns.
Identify and use verbs correctly in sentences.
Identify and use adjectives to describe nouns.

column3: Identify verbs, adjectives, synonyms, and antonyms.

column3: Use verbs, adjectives, and demonstrative pronouns in sentences.
Classify word functions and distinguish synonyms from antonyms.

column3: Identify and write correctly spelled words.

column2: Vocabulary and Word Knowledge: Final Assessment
column3: Demonstrate mastery of all vocabulary concepts.
Distinguish complete sentences from non-sentences.
Arrange words in correct order to form meaningful sentences.

Distinguish declarative from interrogative sentences.
Distinguish imperative from exclamatory sentences.
Combine simple sentences using conjunctions (and, but, or).
Apply capitalization rules and use correct end punctuation.
Use time order words to sequence events.

Write sentences about national pride using correct structure.
Use "because" to explain reasons and causes.
Understand rising and falling intonation patterns.
Demonstrate mastery of all grammar concepts.

column3: Extract key details from a short text.
Arrange story events in chronological order.
Identify and describe characters and setting.

Use text clues to make predictions.

column3: Identify the problem and solution in a narrative.
Compare and contrast characters, events, or ideas.
Use context clues to determine the meaning of unfamiliar words.
Draw conclusions using text evidence and prior knowledge.
Relate ideas from a text to personal experiences.

column3: Summarize a text by identifying the main idea and key details.

Write complete, correctly punctuated simple sentences.
